sections.heading: What this tracker does
sections.text:
The Portfolio Tracker is a single self-contained HTML file that runs entirely in your browser — no server, no installation, no account required. It solves the core challenge of managing a complex digital asset portfolio across multiple wallets, liquidity pools, staking vaults, perpetual hedge positions, and a speculative watchlist — all in one place with live prices from CoinGecko.

Your data never leaves your machine. Everything is stored in your browser's local storage and a linked .json backup file on your own drive. The only external calls made are to CoinGecko (prices), Deribit (implied volatility), Hyperliquid (funding rates), and this help server (content sync only — no portfolio data transmitted).

sections.heading: The seven tabs — what each does
sections.text:
📊 Summary — Portfolio command center. Total value, deployed capital, overall P&L across all areas, and the IL Break-Even Calculator for modeling new positions before committing capital.

🔁 LP Pools — Tracks concentrated liquidity positions (Uniswap v3, Aerodrome, Orca, Beefy vaults). Live pricing, impermanent loss calculation using platform-appropriate math, yield logging, and the Beat HODL? scorecard — the definitive answer to whether LP yield has overcome impermanent loss.

🏦 Vaults — Single-asset yield positions: staking, lending vaults, LSTs. Tracks APY, yield earned, days active, and net result vs entry value.

💼 Holdings — Spot tokens across all wallets and chains. Average cost basis, P&L, allocation %, and per-holding price alerts (buy trigger, stop loss, T1/T2/T3 take-profit ladder).

⚡ Hedge — Calculates optimal perpetual short positions on Hyperliquid to protect BTC, ETH, and SOL exposure at minimum funding cost. IV from Deribit, funding rates from Hyperliquid, and a 5-step formula produce a regime-based hedge ratio. The Performance Chart builds price history across sessions.

📁 History — Archives all closed pools, rebalances, yield logs, and transactions. Full editing capability. Tax-ready CSV export.

💎 GEMs — Watchlist for speculative tokens with buy triggers, stop losses, three take-profit tiers, Vol/MC momentum signals (1hr/24hr/48hr), and 7-day sparklines.

sections.heading: Key concepts to understand
sections.text:
Beat HODL? — The fundamental LP question: has the yield earned by providing liquidity outweighed the impermanent loss vs simply holding the tokens? Shown as ✓ YES / ✗ NO on every pool.

Hedge ratio — The percentage of your crypto exposure protected by a short position. Hard capped at 60% — you always retain meaningful upside exposure. The 5-step formula uses implied volatility, 7-day trend, funding rate, and pool yield to recommend the optimal ratio.

Net carry — Pool yield earned minus annual funding cost on the hedge. Positive carry means the hedge is net profitable. This is the key metric for deciding whether to hedge at all.

Funded From Holdings — When adding capital to a pool or vault, this feature auto-deducts source tokens from Holdings and logs Transfer transactions automatically — keeping all balances correct without manual editing.

Backup envelope — Your .json backup file contains everything: portfolio data, hedge chart history, activation token, Pushover keys, and settings. Linking the backup file on any machine restores the complete tracker instantly.

sections.heading: Getting started — four steps
sections.text:
1. Open in Chrome or Edge — double-click the HTML file. Firefox is not recommended (File System API limitations).

2. Link your backup file — click ⛁ Data ▾ → Link / Change File → select or create a .json file in the same folder. Auto-save activates immediately (every 7 minutes, silently). The status dot turns green when active.

3. Add your first position — go to the tab that matches what you want to track. Click the + button. For LP Pools: enter both token symbols, current quantities, entry value, and your price range.

4. Refresh prices — click ↻ Refresh in the header. Prices update from CoinGecko and the live header bar populates. From here, everything is live and auto-refreshes every 5 minutes.

sections.heading: Data protection
sections.text:
Auto-save — every 7 minutes when a backup file is linked. Also saves automatically when you switch away from the browser tab. Silent — no notification on every save.

Manual save — ⛁ Data ▾ → Save / Export Backup downloads a dated .json backup. The file uses a versioned envelope format containing both your portfolio data and hedge chart history.

After a cache clear — if your browser cache is cleared, localStorage is wiped. Re-open the tracker and link your .json backup file — all data restores immediately including hedge chart history.

Moving to a new machine — copy the entire tracker folder (HTML file + backup .json + help_img/ folder). Open the HTML in Chrome, link the backup file. Activation, settings, Pushover keys, and all data restore automatically.

sections.heading: Common first-time questions
sections.text:
Why isn't a token price loading? CoinGecko may be rate-limited — wait 60 seconds and click ↻ Refresh. If a specific token always shows — , its CoinGecko ID may be wrong — edit the entry and search for the correct ID.

How do I activate the tracker? Your Install ID is shown on the activation screen. Contact the author with your ID to receive a machine-specific activation code. The code is stored in your backup file and restores automatically on any future import.

What is the Hedge tab for? If you hold BTC, ETH, or SOL in pools or vaults, the Hedge tab helps you size a protective short position on Hyperliquid that offsets downside risk while keeping upside exposure. It is entirely optional — all other tabs function independently.

Is my data safe? Yes — no data ever leaves your machine. The only external calls are to CoinGecko (prices), Deribit (IV), Hyperliquid (funding rates), and the Cloudflare Worker (help content — activation verified, no portfolio data transmitted).

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Provides a bird's-eye view of your entire portfolio — total value, deployed capital, overall P&L, and a breakdown by area (Pools, Vaults, Holdings, GEMs). [img:1]

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: All values are calculated in real time from live prices via CoinGecko. The IL Break-Even Calculator lets you model impermanent loss scenarios for any pool type before committing capital.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: No data entry here — this tab reads from all other tabs. Set your Inception Date by clicking the date field to track portfolio-wide performance from a specific start date.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Use the IL Calculator to model new positions before adding them. Click ↻ Refresh in the header to update all live prices.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text: Deployed = what you put in. Current = what it's worth now. Overall P&L = current minus deployed. Each area card shows its own deployed vs current vs P&L.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why does P&L show — ? Prices haven't loaded yet — click Refresh.
Why doesn't it match my pool totals exactly? Pool P&L includes yield; Holdings P&L is price appreciation only.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Tracks all active liquidity pool positions — token quantities, live values, yield harvested, range status, IL vs HODL comparison, and annualized returns.

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: Live prices fetch from CoinGecko. The ratio between Token A and Token B is compared to your set range (Low/High) to determine range status. IL is calculated using the pool model you select (v2, v3, Curve, Balancer).

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Click + Add Pool. Enter both token symbols, current quantities, entry value, and your price range. Use ✎ to edit any row. Click ▶ on a row to log a yield harvest inline.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Log yield harvests regularly using the ▶ expand panel. When rebalancing use ✎ → Rebalance to reset your entry point. Use Close when exiting entirely.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Range badge: In Range · Watch (within 20% of boundary) · Act Now (within 10%) · OUT OF RANGE.
Net/Ann%: Total return including yield, annualized.
Beat HODL? Pool+Yield vs simply holding the tokens.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why is Live Ratio showing — ? A token price failed to load — try Refresh.
What pool model should I use? Uniswap v3/Orca = v3, Uniswap v2/Sushi = v2, Curve/Beefy stable pairs = Curve, Balancer = Balancer.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Tracks single-asset yield positions — staking, lending vaults, LSTs, and any strategy where you deposit one token and earn yield on it over time.

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: Current value = Qty × Live Price. If you enter an APY, it estimates yield earned based on days since entry. You can override this with actual yield logged.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Click + Add Vault. Enter the token, platform, quantity, APY (optional), entry date, and entry value. Update Qty when your staked amount changes. Enter actual Yield Earned $ when you harvest.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Update yield earned periodically. If you add more capital, edit the vault and adjust qty and entry value. Use Close via the Edit modal to archive to History when exiting.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Ann. Return: Combines price appreciation + yield, annualized. EST means it's using your APY estimate rather than logged yield.
Yield APR = pure yield return excluding price movement.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why is Current showing — ? Token price not loaded — check the CoinGecko ID is correct.
My vault shows a loss — why? The token price may have fallen since entry. Entry value reflects what you deposited, not what it's worth now.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Tracks all spot holdings — tokens held in wallets, exchanges, or hardware devices. Shows live price, 24h change, average cost basis, current value, allocation %, and unrealized P&L.

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: Each row is one token/wallet/chain combination. Average cost updates automatically using weighted average when you buy more. The Group button clusters holdings by wallet for a cleaner view. [img:2]

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Click + Add Holding or use Buy/Sell/Swap/Transfer actions on an existing row. For buys, enter qty and price — cost basis is calculated automatically. For stablecoins, price locks at $1.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Use the 🔔 alert button on any row to set price alerts (buy trigger, stop loss, TP1/2/3). Use Swap to record token-to-token trades. Use Transfer to move between wallets while preserving cost basis.[img:4]

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Avg Cost: Your blended entry price across all purchases.
Alloc %: This holding as a percentage of total spot portfolio value.
P&L $/%: Unrealized gain/loss vs your cost basis.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why does a token show — for price? The CoinGecko ID may be wrong — edit and search for the correct token.
How do I record a sale? Use the Sell action on the holding row — it reduces qty and logs the transaction.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Calculates optimal short hedge sizes for BTC, ETH, and SOL to protect your LP and vault positions from downside price risk. Tracks open shorts on Hyperliquid and monitors funding rates.

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: The optimal ratio is calculated from IV (implied volatility), 7-day trend, funding rate, your pool yield APY, and BTC/asset stability signals. Funding rates and IV fetch live from Hyperliquid and Deribit.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Use the slider on each asset card to set your hedge ratio. Click + Open Position to log a short — enter asset, margin ($), leverage, and entry price. Log HL deposits/withdrawals via the HL Deposit/Withdraw button.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Click ↻ Refresh to fetch latest funding rates and IV. Close positions via the Close button on Open Positions. Check the calculation detail (▶ Show) on each card to understand how the ratio was derived.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Funding Rate: Negative = shorts paying longs (costs you). Positive = longs paying shorts (you earn).
Rate / Funding column: Current 8hr rate · cumulative net earned or paid.
Net carry: Annual pool yield minus annual funding cost — positive means hedging is net profitable.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why is funding rate showing — ? Click Refresh — Hyperliquid data fetches on demand.
What does negative net carry mean? Funding cost exceeds pool yield — consider reducing hedge ratio.
What is the Limit Buy checkbox? Signals recovery conviction and reduces the recommended hedge by ~18%.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Archives all closed and rebalanced pool positions, yield harvest logs, and transactions. Your permanent record for performance analysis and tax reporting.

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: Positions are auto-archived when you Close or Rebalance from the LP Pools tab. Yield entries are linked to their pool. The Loss Status flag lets you mark positions where funds were lost to hacks, mismanagement, or theft.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Use ⚙ on any history row to edit dates, name, type, and Loss Status. To mark a loss: click ⚙, set Loss Status (Unrecoverable / Partial / Legal Action), and add a note describing what happened.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Add yield entries to closed pools via ➕ Add Yield Entry. Export to CSV for tax reporting. Update Loss Status if funds are partially recovered or legal proceedings conclude.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Net Result: Exit value + yield - entry value.
Beat HODL? Did pool + yield outperform simply holding the tokens?
Loss badges: ⚠ Unrecoverable · 🔶 Partial · ⚖️ Legal Action · ✅ Recovered.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
How do I record a hack or theft? Close the vault/pool at $0 exit value, then edit the history entry and set Loss Status to Unrecoverable with a note.
Can I change the loss status later? Yes — fully editable at any time via the ⚙ button.

sections.heading: What this tab does
sections.text: Watchlist for speculative tokens — tracks live price, 24h change, Vol/MC momentum, entry price, targets (T1/T2/T3), stop loss, and buy triggers. Sends Pushover alerts when prices hit your levels.

sections.heading: How it functions
sections.text: Prices fetch from CoinGecko. Vol/MC (Volume/Market Cap ratio) is tracked over 1hr, 24hr, and 48hr windows to detect momentum shifts before price moves. Sparklines show 7-day price history at a glance.

sections.heading: How to enter data
sections.text: Click + Add GEM and search for the token. Set your entry price, qty held, and targets. Use the ▶ expand panel on any row to set Stop Loss, T1/T2/T3, and Add trigger inline.

sections.heading: Further actions
sections.text: Set up Pushover in Settings (⚙ icon) for phone alerts. Use ✔ Executed? to confirm TP sells and auto-update your qty. The thesis editor (📝) lets you record your investment case for each token.

sections.heading: How to interpret
sections.text:
Vol/MC columns (1h/24h/48h): % change in volume-to-market-cap ratio vs that window. Rising Vol/MC often precedes price moves.
Alert % badge: Custom mover alert threshold — fires when 24h move exceeds this %.

sections.heading: Common questions
sections.text:
Why is live price showing — ? The CoinGecko ID is wrong — edit and search again.
What's the difference between Alert In and T1? Alert In fires when price drops TO your level (buy). T1/T2/T3 fire when price rises TO your target (sell).
How do I get phone alerts? Install Pushover app, create account, enter User Key and App Token in Settings.

faq.a: All data lives in your browser's localStorage and in a linked backup .json file on your local drive. Nothing is sent to any server. You own your data completely.

faq.q: What happens if I clear my browser cache?
faq.a: localStorage is wiped on a cache clear. If you have a linked backup file, re-open the tracker and link the file again — your data restores automatically. This is why regular Export Backup is important.

faq.q: How do I set up auto-save?
faq.a: Click the ⛁ icon → Link / Change File → select or create a .json file on your drive. Auto-save then runs every 7 minutes while the tracker is open.

CoinGecko has rate limits on the free tier. Wait 60 seconds and click ↻ Refresh. If a specific token shows — permanently, the CoinGecko ID is likely wrong — edit the entry and search for the correct ID.

faq.q: Can I use this on mobile?
faq.a: Yes — the tracker is installable as a PWA (Progressive Web App) on Android and iOS. On mobile Chrome/Safari, use the browser menu → 'Add to Home Screen'. File System auto-save works on Android Chrome; on iOS use Export Backup manually.

faq.q: How do I back up my data?
faq.a: Use ⛁ Data → Save / Export Backup to download a dated .json file. Store it somewhere safe. To restore, open the tracker and use ⛁ Data → Import / Link Backup and select your file.

IL is the difference in value between holding tokens in a liquidity pool vs simply holding them in your wallet. When token prices diverge from your entry ratio, the pool rebalances automatically and you end up with less of the outperforming token. The IL calculator on the Summary tab lets you model this for any scenario.

faq.q: What is the hedge calculator for?
faq.a: If you hold LP positions in volatile assets (BTC, ETH, SOL), a short position on a perp exchange can offset downside price risk. The Hedge tab calculates the optimal short size based on your pool exposure, current implied volatility, and funding rate.

notes: Added Overview tab — full introduction to the tracker
Added What's New section for version 46.99
Help site now receives Overview and What's New content via sync
Hedge chart now persists price history across sessions (IV-adaptive sampling)
Backup file now uses versioned envelope format — includes hedge chart history
HedgeCache feed data no longer written to backup file

46.99.headline: Version 46.99 — What's New
46.99.summary: Hedge chart persistence, GitHub help site, security hardening, and backup improvements.

items.title: Hedge chart now builds history across sessions
items.text: The Hedge Performance Chart accumulates price history between sessions using IV-adaptive sampling — 1, 2, 5, or 10 minute intervals based on current implied volatility, plus a 0.5% spot-move trigger that forces a sample regardless of interval. The 1H / 6H / 1D / 7D / All time windows are now meaningful as history accumulates over days and weeks. History is stored in a dedicated key (HPC_hist_v1) — never mixed with portfolio data — and travels with your backup file.

items.title: Live help documentation site
items.text: A full help site is now live at jdgoldstein.github.io/portfoliotracker-help — searchable, dark mode, sidebar navigation, tab-by-tab documentation, FAQ, and changelog. The tracker fetches help content in the background on startup via an activation-gated Cloudflare Worker. Edit help locally using the ✏ Edit button in the help modal, then click ⬆ Export to GitHub and upload help-content.json to the repo — all users receive updates within one hour.

items.title: Security hardening
items.text: Activation secrets are no longer stored as plain text string literals — assembled at runtime from split arrays. The Demo's universal bypass key (ACT_MASTER) has been completely removed — only machine-specific derived codes accepted. The Demo trial is now triple-stamped (localStorage + IndexedDB + backup file field) to prevent reset via cache clear. demoRestoreTrialStamp() recovers the trial start from any surviving store on activation.

items.title: Per-recipient activation code generator
items.text: Settings → 🔑 Generate User Code. Enter a recipient's Install ID (shown on their activation screen) to generate the unique machine-specific activation code for their machine. Code is copied to clipboard automatically. Keep a record of issued Install IDs — future versions can revoke access by rotating the application secret.

items.title: Versioned backup envelope (schema v2)
items.text: Backup files now use a structured envelope: { schemaVersion: 2, db: {...}, hpcHistory: {...}, _lic, savedAt }. Hedge chart history travels with your backup and restores automatically on import. HedgeCache feed data (IV, funding rates, trend signals) is no longer written to backup files — only user overrides (ratio_*, limitBuy_*, ivSpreadBaseline) are preserved. Legacy v1 flat-format backups fully supported on import.

items.title: Auto-save every 7 minutes, silently
items.text: Auto-save interval reduced from 10 to 7 minutes. No more toast notification on every auto-save tick — the last-saved timestamp is visible in the ⛁ Data menu. Auto-save also correctly strips feed data before writing, matching the clean output of a manual export.

items.title: Overview tab and What's New modal
items.text: The ? Help modal now has a tab navigation bar at the top. The first tab (⛁ Overview) provides a full introduction to the tracker — what each tab does, key concepts, getting started steps, and data protection. On each new version, a What's New modal appears automatically after activation (once per version), summarising the new features with a link to the full changelog.
